> 
**输入：**`nums = [-5,-2,3], queries = [[0,2],[2,0],[1,2]]`

 - 对于 `[0, 2]`，路径 `0 → 1 → 2` 包含一次从下标 0 到 1 的最近移动，代价为 1，以及一次从下标 1 到 2 的移动，代价为 `|-2 - 3| = 5`，总代价为 `1 + 5 = 6`。

 - 对于 `[1, 2]`，从下标 1 直接移动到下标 2 的代价为 `|-2 - 3| = 5`，这是最优的。

-module(solution_solver).
-export([solve/2]).

%% solve(Nums, Queries) -> Ans
%% N <= 10^5, Q <= 10^5. The O(N^2 + QN) complexity derived from the full shortest path search is too high.
%% However, based on the problem description, the shortest path must be found by considering direct jumps combined with the structured "closest" jumps.
%% We implement the derived shortest path logic, assuming the constraints might allow this approach or that this is the intended path if a faster graph algorithm isn't apparent.

solve(Nums, Queries) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % Step 1: Precompute closest(x) for all x
    Closest = precompute_closest(Nums, N),
    
    % Step 2: Compute All-Pairs Shortest Path on G_c (Directed graph: i -> closest[i], weight 1)
    DistClosest = precompute_all_pairs_closest_dist(N, Closest),
    
    % Step 3: Process Queries
    Ans = [solve_query(Nums, N, DistClosest, Closest, Q) || Q <- Queries],
    
    Ans.

precompute_closest(Nums, N) ->
    [ClosestList] = [
        [
            ClosestX || X <- 0..N-1
        ]
    ],
    ClosestList.

ClosestX when X =:= 0 ->
    % Only check x+1
    Y = X + 1,
    % Since N >= 2, Y exists
    Diff = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[Y]),
    BestY = Y,
    ClosestX = BestY;

ClosestX when X =:= N-1 ->
    % Only check x-1
    Y = X - 1,
    % Since N >= 2, Y exists
    Diff = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[Y]),
    BestY = Y,
    ClosestX = BestY;

ClosestX ->
    % Check x-1 and x+1
    X_minus_1 = X - 1,
    X_plus_1 = X + 1,
    
    Diff_minus_1 = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[X_minus_1]),
    Diff_plus_1 = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[X_plus_1]),
    
    if Diff_minus_1 < Diff_plus_1 ->
        ClosestX = X_minus_1;
    else if Diff_plus_1 < Diff_minus_1 ->
        ClosestX = X_plus_1;
    else % Diff_minus_1 == Diff_plus_1
        % Choose smaller index
        ClosestX = X_minus_1
    end.

% Helper for safe list/array access (equivalent to array[i] in JS)
Nums[I] ->
    nth(I+1, Nums, Value),
    Value.

precompute_all_pairs_closest_dist(N, Closest) ->
    [DistClosestList] = [
        [
            DistClosestX || StartNode <- 0..N-1
        ]
    ],
    DistClosestList.

DistClosestX when StartNode =:= 0 ->
    % BFS starting from StartNode
    DistClosestX = bfs_from_node(StartNode, N, Closest).

DistClosestX ->
    % BFS starting from StartNode
    DistClosestX = bfs_from_node(StartNode, N, Closest).

bfs_from_node(StartNode, N, Closest) ->
    % Initialize distances to Infinity (a large number)
    Distances = list_to_array(N, max_integer()),
    
    % Set start distance to 0
    Distances = array_set(Distances, StartNode, 0),
    
    % Queue holds [Node, Distance]
    Queue = [StartNode, 0],
    
    % BFS loop
    bfs_loop(Queue, Distances, Closest, N).

bfs_loop([], Distances, _, _) ->
    array_to_list(Distances);

bfs_loop([Node, Dist] | Tail, Distances, Closest, N) ->
    % Current node is Node. Only outgoing edge is Node -> Closest[Node]
    NextNode = Closest[Node],
    NextDist = Dist + 1,
    
    % Check if NextNode has been visited (i.e., if current recorded distance is smaller than NextDist)
    CurrentDistNextNode = array_get(Distances, NextNode),
    
    if NextDist < CurrentDistNextNode ->
        % Update distance and enqueue
        NewDistances = array_set(Distances, NextNode, NextDist),
        NewQueue = [NextNode, NextDist] ++ Tail,
        bfs_loop(NewQueue, NewDistances, Closest, N);
    else
        % Already found a shorter or equal path
        bfs_loop(Tail, Distances, Closest, N)
    end.


solve_query(Nums, N, DistClosest, Closest, [L, R]) ->
    % 1. Direct jump cost
    MinCost = abs(Nums[L] - Nums[R]),
    
    % 2. Check paths L --(closest)--> K --(direct)--> R
    MinCost = min_cost(MinCost, L, R, DistClosest, Nums, Closest, [K || K <- 0..N-1], 
        fun(K) ->
            Dist_LK = array_get(DistClosest, L, K),
            if Dist_LK =:= max_integer() ->
                -1 % Sentinel for impossible path
            else
                Cost = Dist_LK + abs(Nums[K] - Nums[R]),
                Cost
            end
        end
    ),

    % 3. Check paths L --(direct)--> K --(closest)--> R
    MinCost = min_cost(MinCost, L, R, DistClosest, Nums, Closest, [K || K <- 0..N-1], 
        fun(K) ->
            Dist_KR = array_get(DistClosest, K, R),
            if Dist_KR =:= max_integer() ->
                -1
            else
                Cost = abs(Nums[L] - Nums[K]) + Dist_KR,
                Cost
            end
        end
    ).

%% solve(Nums, Queries) -> Ans
%% N <= 10^5, Q <= 10^5. The O(N^2 + QN) complexity derived from the full shortest path search is too high.
%% However, based on the problem description, the shortest path must be found by considering direct jumps combined with the structured "closest" jumps.
%% We implement the derived shortest path logic, assuming the constraints might allow this approach or that this is the intended path if a faster graph algorithm isn't apparent.

solve(Nums, Queries) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % Step 1: Precompute closest(x) for all x
    Closest = precompute_closest(Nums, N),
    
    % Step 2: Compute All-Pairs Shortest Path on G_c (Directed graph: i -> closest[i], weight 1)
    DistClosest = precompute_all_pairs_closest_dist(N, Closest),
    
    % Step 3: Process Queries
    Ans = [solve_query(Nums, N, DistClosest, Closest, Q) || Q <- Queries],
    
    Ans.

precompute_closest(Nums, N) ->
    [ClosestList] = [
        [
            ClosestX || X <- 0..N-1
        ]
    ],
    ClosestList.

ClosestX when X =:= 0 ->
    % Only check x+1
    Y = X + 1,
    % Since N >= 2, Y exists
    Diff = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[Y]),
    BestY = Y,
    ClosestX = BestY;

ClosestX when X =:= N-1 ->
    % Only check x-1
    Y = X - 1,
    % Since N >= 2, Y exists
    Diff = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[Y]),
    BestY = Y,
    ClosestX = BestY;

ClosestX ->
    % Check x-1 and x+1
    X_minus_1 = X - 1,
    X_plus_1 = X + 1,
    
    Diff_minus_1 = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[X_minus_1]),
    Diff_plus_1 = abs(Nums[X] - Nums[X_plus_1]),
    
    if Diff_minus_1 < Diff_plus_1 ->
        ClosestX = X_minus_1;
    else if Diff_plus_1 < Diff_minus_1 ->
        ClosestX = X_plus_1;
    else % Diff_minus_1 == Diff_plus_1
        % Choose smaller index
        ClosestX = X_minus_1
    end.

% Helper for safe list/array access (equivalent to array[i] in JS)
Nums[I] ->
    nth(I+1, Nums, Value),
    Value.

precompute_all_pairs_closest_dist(N, Closest) ->
    [DistClosestList] = [
        [
            DistClosestX || StartNode <- 0..N-1
        ]
    ],
    DistClosestList.

DistClosestX when StartNode =:= 0 ->
    % BFS starting from StartNode
    DistClosestX = bfs_from_node(StartNode, N, Closest).

    % BFS starting from StartNode
    DistClosestX = bfs_from_node(StartNode, N, Closest).

    % Initialize distances to Infinity (a large number)
    Distances = list_to_array(N, max_integer()),
    
    % Set start distance to 0
    Distances = array_set(Distances, StartNode, 0),
    
    % Queue holds [Node, Distance]
    Queue = [StartNode, 0],
    
    % BFS loop
    bfs_loop(Queue, Distances, Closest, N).

    array_to_list(Distances);

bfs_loop([Node, Dist] | Tail, Distances, Closest, N) ->
    % Current node is Node. Only outgoing edge is Node -> Closest[Node]
    NextNode = Closest[Node],
    NextDist = Dist + 1,
    
    % Check if NextNode has been visited (i.e., if current recorded distance is smaller than NextDist)
    CurrentDistNextNode = array_get(Distances, NextNode),
    
    if NextDist < CurrentDistNextNode ->

        NewDistances = array_set(Distances, NextNode, NextDist),
        NewQueue = [NextNode, NextDist] ++ Tail,

        bfs_loop(Tail, Distances, Closest, N)
    end.

    lists:split(List, Index, [Before, After]),
